item.title: Holi festival celebrated
item.description: Hindu community members on Tuesday celebrated Holi, a religious festival of colours, across the country with much enthusiasm and religious fervour....

item.title: Protests against attacks on Iran continue
item.description: Differentpoliticalparties in Bangladesh on Tuesday continued protesting at the joint attacks on Iran by United States and Israel....Foreign affairs analysis

item.title: Yunus to get VVIP protection for one year
item.description: Former chief adviser to the interim government Professor Muhammad Yunus will remain under security coverage by the Special Security Force for one year after being declared a ‘Very Very Important Person’....

item.title: Cultural personality Mukul Bose dies
item.description: Cultural personality Mukul Das passed away in Barishal Sher-e-Bangla Medical College Hospital at about 3:30am on Tuesday. He was 75....

item.title: NCP MP besieged in N’ganj
item.description: National Citizen Party leader and lawmaker for the Narayanganj-4 constituency, Abdullah Al Amin, was besieged by an agitating group on Tuesday after he had labelled Bangladesh Knitwear Manufacturers and Exporters Association president Mohammad Hatem a ‘fascist’s ally’....Country-specific news

item.title: BFIU seeks bank info of NCP leader Asif Bhuyain
item.description: The Bangladesh Financial Intelligence Unit has instructed all banks to immediately provide account details and transaction records of Asif Mahmud Shojib Bhuyain, former adviser to the Ministry of Local Government, Rural Development (LGRD) and Cooperatives....

item.title: Thousands of Afghans displaced by Pakistan conflict
item.description: More than 8,000 Afghans have been forced from their homes by fighting with Pakistani forces along the border in recent days, the Taliban government said Tuesday....

item.title: Ex-army chief tells ICT graft took place during his tenure
item.description: Former chief of army staff Iqbal Karim Bhuiyan told the International Crimes Tribunal-1 on Tuesday that corruption in defence procurement took place during his tenure, but denied any personal involvement....

item.title: Does ‘mutual understanding’ legalise extortion?
item.description: WHEN robbers and dacoits hold guns to the heads of defenceless people and demand money, the victims immediately negotiate with the criminals to protect their lives. In order to avoid bodily harm or even death, they willingly give the scoundrels whatever they want. Such a compromise/agreement results from instant intuition and is driven by survival instinct. Since the......

item.title: 266 factories likely to face labour unrest over dues ahead of Eid
item.description: Concerns are mounting over potential labour unrest, as according to findings by law enforcement agencies, around 266 factories, including textile and readymade garment units, might fail to pay wages and festival allowances ahead of Eid-ul-Fitr....

item.title: That longing for regime change
item.description: THE theme of regime change reminds me of the two sisters at Aligarh Muslim University who were erudite teachers and voluble in their left-oriented feminism. Professor Waheed Akhtar, a less stridently liberal member of the Urdu faculty, was asked which of the two he found less reprimanding in discussing disputes over a point of view. ‘The one who isn’t present,’ he......

item.title: When analysts get it wrong
item.description: POLITICAL analysis exists in the tension between evidence and uncertainty. Analysts observe signals, interpret behaviour, examine institutional incentives and attempt to map plausible futures. Yet politics is not laboratory science. Outcomes are shaped not only by structures but by decisions taken under pressure, negotiations conducted out of public view and moments......

item.title: Path to water survival
item.description: CLIMATE change has delivered the Ganges basin its worst droughts in 1,300 years, while atmospheric rivers drive catastrophic floods. Extreme monsoon events have quadrupled, with these rivers responsible for nearly three-quarters of all floods. The 1996 Ganges Water Treaty now operates in a climate regime that no longer exists......

item.title: Illegal US-Israeli attack on Iran is also an assault on UN
item.description: ON FEBRUARY 16, 2026, one of us (Jeffrey Sachs) sent a letter to the UN Security Council warning that the United States was on the verge of tearing up the United Nations Charter. That warning has now come to pass. The United States and Israel have launched an unprovoked war against Iran in flagrant violation of Article 2(4) of the Charter, without authorisation from......

item.title: ME war likely to hit labour migration
item.description: Bangladesh’s labour migration and remittance inflow could face a serious setback if the Iran war involving the Middle East lingers as the country’s overseas employment is heavily dependent on Gulf nations....

item.title: Losing mango belt threatens more than fruit
item.description: THE land coverage of mango orchards has declined in Rajshahi and Chapainawabganj this year as farmers increasingly convert orchard land into fish ponds. This has raised concern about the change in land-use patterns in the traditional mango belt. Department of Agricultural Extension data show that mango orchard areas in Rajshahi fell by 541 hectares to 19,062......

item.title: Time-bound, decisive steps must to improve law and order
item.description: THIS is disconcerting that the government has not been able to restore law and order although it announced law and order as its utmost priority on assuming office on February 17. On March 2, incidents of murder, mob violence, rape and robbery were reported. An 11-year-old boy was found strangled and brutalised in a paddy field at Paba in Rajshahi district. A man was.....

item.title: Unbeaten SA face ‘fresh start’: Markram
item.description: South Africa captain Aiden Markram on Tuesday said his team’s win over New Zealand in the group phase will count for nothing in the T20 World Cup semi-final, which will be a ‘completely fresh start’......

item.title: Underdogs New Zealand eye T20 World Cup final
item.description: Underdogs New Zealand back themselves against anyone in ‘one-off games’, declared captain Mitchell Santner Tuesday on the eve of their T20 World Cup semi-final against South Africa in Kolkata......

item.title: War rages on in ME
item.description: Drones hit the US embassy in Riyadh on Tuesday as Iran hit back at industrial and diplomatic targets across the Middle East and Washington warned its citizens to evacuate the entire region....

item.title: West Indies, Zimbabwe stuck in India because of travel chaos
item.description: The West Indies and Zimbabwe cricket squads and support staff at thw T20 World Cup have been stranded in India, unable to leave because of airspace closures and travle chaos caused by the war in the Middle East......

item.title: Show me proof, then I won’t play cricket: Anamul
item.description: Anamul Haque Bijoy sought proof of his wrongdoing; otherwise, he wanted his respect and the opportunity to play while speaking with media at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium in Mirpur on Tuesday......

item.title: Getafe deal flat Real La Liga title race blow
item.description: Real Madrid fell to a damaging 1-0 defeat by Getafe at home in La Liga on Monday, leaving them four points behind leaders Barcelona......

item.title: 4 held over human trafficking, visa scam
item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion early Tuesday arrested four alleged members of an organised human trafficking syndicate, including its alleged ringleader, on charges of sending people abroad using fake visas....

item.title: KUSHTIA DC WITHDRAWAL: BNP, Jamaat at loggerheads
item.description: The activists of the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami continued demonstrations in Kushtia on Tuesday demanding the cancellation of the government’s Sunday order to withdraw the district deputy commissioner, while the Bangladesh Nationalist Party formed a human chain in the district town to......

item.title: Experts suggest prioritising health sector reforms
item.description: Health experts on Tuesday recommended policymakers to execute priority-based reforms as proposed by the recent-past interim government-formed Health Sector Reform Commission....

item.title: Tungipara election officer suspended over NID irregularities
item.description: The election commission has suspended the upazila election officer of Tungipara in Gopalganj after allegations of irregularities in correcting National Identity cards were proven through a departmental investigation....

item.title: Japan, UNICEF partner to support Rohingya children
item.description: The government of Japan and UNICEF have signed a new agreement designed to deliver life‑saving support to Rohingya children and their families in Cox’s Bazar and Bhasan Char....

item.title: Odhikar condemns harassment of ex-adviser Adilur at HSIA
item.description: Human rights organisation Odhikar on Tuesday condemned the alleged harassment of former housing and public works adviser Adilur Rahman Khan by the immigration officials at Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport on Monday afternoon while he was travelling to Nepal....

item.title: Agriculture ministry, three divisions get new secretaries on contract
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led government on Tuesday appointed new secretaries to the agriculture ministry and three key divisions on one-year contracts....Breaking news alerts

item.title: Almost a third of women in EU experience violence: report
item.description: Nearly one in three women in the European Union is subjected to some form of violence during her lifetime, the bloc’s rights agency said Tuesday, warning that gender-based violence is ‘widespread, increasingly digital and overlooked’....

item.title: Dhaka seeks UK support for UNGA presidency
item.description: Bangladesh foreign minister Khalilur Rahman sought the United Kingdom’s support for Bangladesh’s candidature for the presidency of the upcoming 81st session of the United Nations General Assembly....

item.title: AI disinformation turns Nepal polls into ‘digital battleground’
item.description: Slick AI-generated disinformation has flooded election campaigns in Nepal, which votes Thursday in the first polls since deadly protests triggered by a brief ban on social media overthrew the government....

item.title: Local govt polls after party symbol use issue resolved
item.description: Local government, rural development and cooperatives affairs state minister Mir Shahe Alam MP on Tuesday said that local government elections would not be organised until the issue of using party symbols in the polls was resolved....Top news summaries

item.title: Dhaka Polytechnic asks students to vacate hall as JCD, Shibir clash
item.description: Authorities of the Dhaka Polytechnic Institute asked students to vacate its residential Latif Hall in the capital by Tuesday evening after a violent clash between Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal and Islami Chhatra Shibir....

item.title: 10-day fair on handloom begins Mar 8
item.description: A 10-day exhibition and fair on the Traditional Handloom Industry of Bangladesh will be held from March 8 to 17 at the gallery on the 2nd floor and the open space on the 4th floor of Bengal Foundation....

item.title: GAU holds workshop on transparent grading system
item.description: Gazipur Agricultural University held a daylong training session on rubric-based evaluation and transparent grading at its Institutional Quality Assurance Cell on Tuesday....Country-specific news

item.title: Sugarcane cultivation declines by 52pc in Rajshahi over past decade
item.description: Sugarcane cultivation in the Rajshahi agricultural region has declined by nearly 52 per cent over the past decade, as concerns are raised whether the crop will sustain in the future in one of the country’s known sugarcane-growing belts....

item.title: Dol colours JnU campus
item.description: Students at Jagannath University marked Dol Purnima with vibrant celebrations on Tuesday, as members of the Hindu community gathered to observe the spring festival in a display of color, music, and shared joy....

item.title: CHT TREATY IMPLEMENTATION: Inclusion in 180-day programme demanded
item.description: Leaders of Parbatya Chattogram Chukti Bastabayan Andolan (Chittagong Hill Tracts accord implementation movement) called on the government to include the implementation of the CHT peace accord in its 180-day programme....Geographic Reference

item.title: Prime accused held over Sitakunda girl killing
item.description: Police on Tuesday said that the prime accused in the Chattogram minor girl killing case was arrested in Kumira of Chattogram....

item.title: Visitors, titles rise, not sale
item.description: A large number of visitors thronged the Amar Ekushey Book Fair on Tuesday, but sales were very low while the number of new titles increased, said the publishers....
